critic_raw:
## ML Critique: Readmission Model with Sequence Embeddings (Run 3)

**Issue 1 — Readmitted flag in sequence creates label-based shortcut**

The 6-feature sequence includes readmitted (0/1) for each prior encounter. This is a direct encoding of the target variable for historical admissions. The end-to-end jointly-trained embedding will learn to weight this flag strongly, potentially masking whether the temporal clinical trajectory (LOS, diagnoses, comorbidities) adds any value beyond 'this patient has been readmitted before.' Ablation required.

**Issue 2 — ECE model selection conflicts with discrimination hypothesis**

Section 4 uses ECE for early stopping; Section 6 uses primary metric (ECE) for model selection. ECE can be reduced by collapsing to mean prediction — this would pass the ECE criterion while destroying discrimination. The hypothesis is about whether sequence features improve readmission prediction, which requires discrimination metrics for selection.
defender_raw:
## Design Defense: Readmission Model (Run 3)

Both issues valid. Issue 1: ablation needed (end-to-end training + readmitted flag = potential shortcut). Issue 2: ECE early stopping can terminate before AUROC plateaus — concrete selection mismatch. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication — isolated_debate, Run 3

Both issues acknowledged. Verdict: critique_wins.
